free_text: Quadrupole and hexadecapole moments of 232Th and 238U
from inelastic scattering of 65 MeV polarized protons
Main reference. See also TITLE and AUTHOR.
- Chemical-form of target: ThO2 and U3O8
- Backing is nickel. Aluminum (0.10 mg/cm2 for 233Th
and 0.20 mg/cm2 for 238U) also used for very forward
- Solid angle of detector is 1.4 to 2.5 msr.
- Detector resolution: 20-22 keV and 24-26 keV at
forward and backward angles of 238U case
published in MSK/A are superseded by data published in
PR/C. (2) The chemical form of uranium target is U3O8
which is considered in determination of numbers of
target uranium nuclei in samples. The chemical form
printed in PR/C (UO2) is not correct.
to determine number of oxygen nuclei in samples
